Weymouth and Gill.


The special committee, consisting
of President Boyd and Councilman
Pratt, will receive the proposition
from Mr.. Tannatt this afternoon.


In the matter of purchasing a fire
steamer the recommendatio of the
finance committee will probably be
fought by the water meter gang and
the councilmanic representatives Of
the tall tower. but it is expected that
the order will be made.


Another big fight is expected to-
night when the streetcar franchise
for the new high bridge over Hang
man creek is brought up. The WASH
ington Water Power company applied
for the franchise and the matter was
blocked for the time being through a
suggestion that the Spokane Trac-
tion company would also want to use
the bridge eventually. Since that time
the agents of the Water Power COM
pany have been busy working for an
exclusive franchise. The Graves in
terests are fighting the franchise on
the ground that it should not be ex-
elusive, and more trouble is looked
for.
